---
title: WGCNA Analysis
output: html_document
---

```{r}
library(WGCNA)
library(dplyr)
library(tibble)
library(readr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(DESeq2)

enableWGCNAThreads()
set.seed(1)
```

```{r}
METADATA_PATH <- "combined_metadata.tsv"
DATA_PATHS <- list(
  burgos_dbgap = "burgos_dbgap/burgos_dbgap_counts.txt",
  silver_seq = "silver_seq/silver_seq_counts.txt",
  toden = "toden/toden_counts.txt"
)
STUDIES <- names(DATA_PATHS)
```

```{r}
get_study <- function(study) {
    # load metadata
    metadata <- read_tsv(METADATA_PATH, na = "", show_col_types = FALSE) %>%
        filter(study == !!study) %>%
        filter(type != "CSF") %>%
        drop_na(age) %>%
        transmute(
            sample_id = as.character(sample_id),
            subject_id = as.character(subject_id),
            group = if_else(group == "AD", 1, 0),
            age = as.numeric(age),
            sex = if_else(sex == "male", 1, 0)
        ) %>%
        column_to_rownames("sample_id")

    # load data
    data <- read_tsv(DATA_PATHS[[study]], skip = 1, col_names = FALSE, show_col_types = FALSE)
    colnames <- colnames(read_tsv(DATA_PATHS[[study]], n_max = 0, show_col_types = FALSE))
    colnames(data) <- c("genes", colnames)
    data <- data %>% column_to_rownames(var = "genes")

    # filter genes and samples
    samples <- intersect(rownames(metadata), colnames(data))
    genes <- rownames(data)[rowSums(data >= 1) >= 0.25 * ncol(data)]
    data <- as.matrix(data)[genes, samples]
    metadata <- metadata[samples, ]

    # average over subjects
    subjects <- unique(metadata$subject_id)
    data <- sapply(subjects, function(s) {
        cols <- which(metadata$subject_id == s)
        round(rowMeans(data[, cols, drop = FALSE]))
    })
    rownames(data) <- rownames(data)
    colnames(data) <- subjects
    metadata <- metadata[match(subjects, metadata$subject_id), ]
    metadata$subject_id <- NULL
    rownames(metadata) <- subjects
    
    # normalize data
    data <- t(vst(data, blind = TRUE))

    # quality control
    good <- goodSamplesGenes(data)
    data <- data[good$goodSamples, good$goodGenes]
    metadata <- metadata[good$goodSamples, ]
    var <- apply(data, 2, var)
    mask <- order(var, decreasing = TRUE)[1:15000]
    data <- data[, mask]
    
    list(data = data, metadata = metadata)
}
```

```{r}
studies <- lapply(STUDIES, get_study)
names(studies) <- STUDIES
```

```{r, fig.width = 18, fig.height = 6}
for (study in STUDIES) {
    tree <- hclust(dist(studies[[study]]$data), method = "average")
    plot(tree, main = paste0("Sample Clustering: ", study),
       sub = "", xlab = "", cex = 0.6)
}
```

```{r, fig.width = 12, fig.height = 6}
powers <- setNames(numeric(length(STUDIES)), STUDIES)

for (study in STUDIES) {
    sft <- pickSoftThreshold(studies[[study]]$data, 
        powerVector = c(1:10, seq(12, 20, 2)), verbose = 0)
    powers[study] <- sft$powerEstimate
    
    par(mfrow = c(1, 2))
    plot(sft$fitIndices$Power, -sign(sft$fitIndices$slope) * sft$fitIndices$SFT.R.sq,
        xlab = "Soft power", ylab = "Scale-free R^2",
        main = paste0("Scale Independence: ", study))
    abline(h = 0.85, col = "red")
    plot(sft$fitIndices$Power, sft$fitIndices$mean.k.,
        xlab = "Soft power", ylab = "Mean connectivity",
        main = paste0("Mean Connectivity: ", study))
}
print(powers)
```

```{r}
cor <- WGCNA::cor

get_network <- function(study, data, power) {
    net <- blockwiseModules(data, power = power, 
        numericLabels = TRUE, pamRespectsDendro = FALSE,
        mergeCutHeight = if_else(study == "silver_seq", 0.4, 0.15), 
        minModuleSize = 30, verbose = 0)
    colors <- labels2colors(net$colors)
    MEs <- orderMEs(moduleEigengenes(data, colors)$eigengenes)
    list(net = net, colors = colors, MEs = MEs)
}
for (study in STUDIES) {
    studies[[study]]$network = get_network(study,
        studies[[study]]$data, powers[study])
    print(table(studies[[study]]$network$colors))
}
```

```{r, fig.width = 8, fig.height = 8}
get_module_trait <- function(data, metadata, MEs) {
    cors  <- cor(MEs, metadata, use = "pairwise.complete.obs")
    pvals <- corPvalueStudent(cors, nrow(data))
    list(cor = cors, p = pvals)
}
for (study in STUDIES) {
    studies[[study]]$module_trait = get_module_trait(studies[[study]]$data, 
        studies[[study]]$metadata, studies[[study]]$network$MEs)
    text <- paste0(signif(studies[[study]]$module_trait$cor, 2), 
        "\n(", signif(studies[[study]]$module_trait$p, 1), ")")
    dim(text) <- dim(studies[[study]]$module_trait$cor)
    
    par(mar = c(6, 9, 3, 1))
    labeledHeatmap(
        Matrix = studies[[study]]$module_trait$cor, 
        xLabels = colnames(studies[[study]]$module_trait$cor),
        yLabels = rownames(studies[[study]]$module_trait$cor), 
        ySymbols = rownames(studies[[study]]$module_trait$cor),
        colorLabels = FALSE, colors = blueWhiteRed(50),
        textMatrix = text, setStdMargins = FALSE, 
        cex.text = 0.6, zlim = c(-1, 1), 
        main = paste0("Module Trait Relationships: ", study)
    )
}
```

```{r}
common_genes <- Reduce(intersect, lapply(studies, function(s) colnames(s$data)))
reference_study <- "toden"
test_studies <- setdiff(STUDIES, reference_study)
ordered_studies <- c(reference_study, test_studies)
ordered_data <- lapply(ordered_studies, function(s)
    list(data = studies[[s]]$data[, common_genes]))
names(ordered_data) <- ordered_studies

reference_colors <- studies[[reference_study]]$network$colors
names(reference_colors) <- colnames(studies[[reference_study]]$data)
colors <- list(reference_colors[common_genes])
names(colors) <- reference_study

preserved_modules <- modulePreservation(ordered_data, colors)
```
```{r, fig.width = 8, fig.height = 4}
z_summaries <- preserved_modules$preservation$Z[[paste0("ref.", reference_study)]]
summary <- lapply(test_studies, function(s) {
  stats <- z_summaries[[paste0("inColumnsAlsoPresentIn.", s)]]
  tibble(module = rownames(stats), !!paste0("Z_", s) := stats$Zsummary.pres)
})

summary[[3]] <- tibble(module = sub("^ME", "", 
    rownames(studies[[reference_study]]$module_trait$cor)),
    cor = studies[[reference_study]]$module_trait$cor[, "group"],
    p = studies[[reference_study]]$module_trait$p[, "group"])

summary <- Reduce(function(a, b) full_join(a, b, by = c("module")), 
  summary) %>% filter(!module %in% c("gold", "grey")) %>% rowwise() %>%
  mutate(Z_avg = mean(c_across(starts_with("Z_")))) %>% ungroup()

ggplot(summary, aes(x = p, y = Z_avg, label = module)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 2, linetype = "dashed") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0.05, linetype = "dashed") +
  geom_point(aes(color = module), size = 3, show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_text(vjust = -0.8, size = 3) + scale_x_log10() +
  scale_color_manual(values = setNames(summary$module, summary$module)) +
  labs(y = "Average ZSummary", x = "Correlation P-Value")
```
```{r}
MODULES <- c("cyan", "blue", "red", "turquoise", "brown", "magenta")
kME <- signedKME(studies[[reference_study]]$data, studies[[reference_study]]$network$MEs)

hub_genes <- lapply(MODULES, function(m) {
  genes <- colnames(studies[[reference_study]]$data)[colors[[reference_study]] == m]
  kme_column <- paste0("kME", m)
  tibble(module = m, gene = genes, kME  = kME[genes, kme_column]) %>%
    arrange(desc(kME)) %>% slice_head(n = 3)
})
hub_genes <- bind_rows(hub_genes)
hub_genes
writeLines(unlist(hub_genes$gene), "WGCNA_hub_genes.txt")
```
